- Cocoa processing arbitrice: With bean prices at 20-month lows, margins for local grinding, butter/powder production, and chocolate manufacturing are widening — Ghanaian processors should be locking in supply now
- Rubber supply gap: Natural rubber at 234.40 cents/kg, up 46.50% YoY and at highs since January 2017 — West African rubber plantations (Liberia, Nigeria, Côte d'Ivoire) are well-positioned to capture demand displaced by Thai/Indonesian weather disruptions
- Rice import substitution: ECOWAS's $5bn annual rice import bill remains the region's most bankable agri-investment thesis — milling, seed systems, and warehouse infrastructure are all underinvested
- Cocoa price trajectory: Trading Economics forecasts $3,862/t by end-Q2 and $3,259/t in 12 months — further declines will pressure COCOBOD's ability to maintain the GH¢2,587/bag producer price
- West African monsoon onset: Thailand and Indonesia face weather disruptions; West Africa's June–September cocoa mid-crop harvest will be the next major supply signal
- Ghana cedi at GH\u00a211.79/$: Further depreciation raises input costs (fertiliser, machinery) for farmers even as export commodity prices in dollars soften
- COCOBOD 2025/26 pricing policy: Whether the GH¢2,587/bag (approximately $2,190/t at current rates) remains competitive if the cedi strengthens or global prices recover

- Mobile money\u2013stablecoin integrati: with MTN MoMo and Vodafone Cash deeply embedded, the first mover to bridge stablecoins into Ghana's mobile money ecosystem has a significant distribution advantage
- USDC/USDT payout infrastructure for re: Western Union's USDPT on Solana and Circle's Sasai partnership validate the model; local operators can undercut traditional fees of 6–10%
- Ghana SEC sandbox progression: 11 companies admitted; full licences possible after 6 months — watch for which platforms graduate and when
- Nigeria SEC VASP licensing framework: Nigeria dominates African crypto activity ($92B+ in onchain value, Jul 2024–Jun 2025); regulatory clarity could unlock institutional capital
- Western Union USDPT stablecoin launch: if successful, this becomes the largest stablecoin distribution experiment in African remittance history
- Rwanda's draft VASP framework: moving through legislature; could become East Africa's regulatory template
- e-Cedi (Ghana) and e-franc rwandais (R: both CBDCs in advanced proof-of-concept stages; watch for pilot announcements that could reshape the stablecoin competitive landscape

- Rice import substitution: $5bn annual gap, ECOWAS backing, World Bank $1.2bn FSRP funding — local milling, seed systems, and warehouse infrastructure are bankable
- Ghanaian-owned mining equipment supply: Damang Gold Mine's $250m fleet purchase signals demand for local equipment leasing, maintenance, and parts distribution
- Dangote Refinery surplus: 650k bpd capacity expanding to 700k bpd by 2028 — regional fuel import displacement creates distribution and trading opportunities across West Africa
